commission_recipient.description: Address to receive the commission fees

curve_type.description:
enum to store the curve parameters used for this contract
if you want to add a custom Curve, you should make a new contract that imports this one.
write a custom `instantiate`, and then dispatch `your::execute` -> `cw20_bonding::do_execute`
with your custom curve as a parameter (and same with `query` -> `do_query`)

decimals.description:
number of decimal places of the supply token, needed for proper curve math.
If it is eg. BTC, where a balance of 10^8 means 1 BTC, then use 8 here.

reserve_decimals.description:
number of decimal places for the reserve token, needed for proper curve math.
Same format as decimals above, eg. if it is uatom, where 1 unit is 10^-6 ATOM, use 6 here

reserve_denom.description: this is the reserve token denom (only support native for now)

symbol.description: symbol / ticker of the supply token

constant.description: Constant always returns `value * 10^-scale` as spot price

linear.description: Linear returns `slope * 10^-scale * supply` as spot price

square_root.description: SquareRoot returns `slope * 10^-scale * supply^0.5` as spot price

definitions.name: cosmwasm_std_math_uint128_Uint128
cosmwasm_std_math_decimal_Decimal
definitions.description:
A fixed-point decimal value with 18 fractional digits, i.e. Decimal(1_000_000_000_000_000_000) == 1.0

The greatest possible value that can be represented is 340282366920938463463.374607431768211455 = (2^128 - 1) / 10^18

burn.description: Implements CW20. Burn is a base message to destroy tokens forever

burn_from.description: Implements CW20 "approval" extension. Destroys tokens forever

buy.description:
Buy will attempt to purchase as many supply tokens as possible.
You must send only reserve tokens in that message

decrease_allowance.description:
Implements CW20 "approval" extension. Lowers the spender's access of tokens
from the owner's (env.sender) account by amount. If expires is Some(), overwrites current
allowance expiration with this one.

increase_allowance.description:
Implements CW20 "approval" extension. Allows spender to access an additional amount tokens
from the owner's (env.sender) account. If expires is Some(), overwrites current allowance
expiration with this one.

send.description:
Implements CW20.  Send is a base message to transfer tokens to a contract and trigger an action
on the receiving contract.

send_from.description:
Implements CW20 "approval" extension. Sends amount tokens from owner -> contract
if `env.sender` has sufficient pre-approval.

transfer.description: Implements CW20. Transfer is a base message to move tokens to another account without triggering actions

transfer_from.description:
Implements CW20 "approval" extension. Transfers amount tokens from owner -> recipient
if `env.sender` has sufficient pre-approval.

definitions.name: cosmwasm_std_math_uint128_Uint128
cosmwasm_std_binary_Binary
definitions.description:
Binary is a wrapper around Vec<u8> to add base64 de/serialization
with serde. It also adds some helper methods to help encode inline.

This is only needed as serde-json-{core,wasm} has a horrible encoding for Vec<u8>.
See also <https://github.com/CosmWasm/cosmwasm/blob/main/docs/MESSAGE_TYPES.md>.

definitions.name: cw_utils_expiration_Expiration
definitions.description:
Expiration represents a point in time when some event happens.
It can compare with a BlockInfo and will return is_expired() == true
once the condition is hit (and for every block in the future)

never.description: Never will never expire. Used to express the empty variant

definitions.name: cosmwasm_std_timestamp_Timestamp

allowance.description:
Implements CW20 "allowance" extension.
Returns how much spender can use from owner account, 0 if unset.

balance.description: Implements CW20. Returns the current balance of the given address, 0 if unset.

curve_info.description: Returns the reserve and supply quantities, as well as the spot price to buy 1 token

token_info.description: Implements CW20. Returns metadata on the contract - name, decimals, supply, etc.

definitions.name: cosmwasm_std_math_uint128_Uint128
cw_utils_expiration_Expiration
definitions.description:
Expiration represents a point in time when some event happens.
It can compare with a BlockInfo and will return is_expired() == true
once the condition is hit (and for every block in the future)

never.description: Never will never expire. Used to express the empty variant

The greatest possible value that can be represented is 340282366920938463463.374607431768211455 = (2^128 - 1) / 10^18
